title: Annex B: Indicative list of Sustainable Farming Incentive actions available in Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier
body: "Annex B: Indicative list of Sustainable Farming Incentive actions available in Countryside Stewardship Higher Tier  Table 1 sets out an indicative list of Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI) actions that may be available to apply for in CS Higher Tier. We will confirm the list of actions this summer.  These actions are available where:     CS Higher Tier actions need to be done in combination with SFI actions to deliver the intended outcomes, and in some cases may need to be tailored to the specific site  they are supplements that support the delivery of other actions   Table 1: Indicative list of SFI actions available in CS Higher Tier     Action Annual payment     Manage hedgerows £13 per 100 metres for one side   Buffer in-field ponds and ditches on arable land £681 per hectare   Buffer in-field ponds and ditches on improved grassland £311 per hectare   Pollen and nectar flower mix £739 per hectare   Bumblebird mix £747 per hectare   Winter bird food on arable and horticultural land £853 per hectare   Supplementary winter bird food £732 per tonne (maximum 1 tonne for every 2 hectares of CAHL2)   Nesting plots for lapwing £765 per hectare   Basic overwinter stubble £58 per hectare   Enhanced overwinter stubble £589 per hectare   Whole crop spring cereals and overwinter stubble £596 per hectare   Unharvested cereal headland £1,072 per hectare   Low input harvested cereal crop £354 per hectare   Cultivated areas for arable plants £660 per hectare   Manage woodland edges on arable land £428 per hectare   Manage rough grazing for birds £121 per hectare   Manage priority habitat species-rich grassland (endorsed) £646 per hectare   Manage grassland with very low nutrient inputs £151 per hectare   Supplement: Haymaking £157 per hectare   Supplement: Haymaking (late cut) £187 per hectare   Supplement: Lenient grazing £28 per hectare   Supplement: Cattle grazing (non-moorland) £59 per hectare   Create scrub and open habitat mosaics £588 per hectare   Manage scrub and open habitat mosaics £350 per hectare   Control scrub on historic and archaeological features £215 per hectare   Manage historic and archaeological features on grassland £55 per hectare   Maintain designed or engineered waterbodies £2,512 per hectare   Flower-rich grass margins, blocks, or in-field strips £798 per hectare   Shepherding livestock on moorland (no required stock removal period) £33 per hectare   Shepherding livestock on moorland (remove stock for at least 4 months) £43 per hectare   Shepherding livestock on moorland (remove stock for at least 6 months) £45 per hectare   Shepherding livestock on moorland (remove stock for at least 8 months) £48 per hectare   Supplement: Keep cattle and ponies on moorland (minimum 30% GLU) £7 per hectare   Supplement: Keep cattle and ponies on moorland (minimum 70% GLU) £18 per hectare   Supplement: Keep cattle and ponies on moorland (100% GLU) £23 per hectare   Moderate livestock grazing on moorland £20 per hectare   Low livestock grazing on moorland £53 per hectare   Limited livestock grazing on moorland £66 per hectare   Supplementary winter bird food (organic land) £935 per tonne (maximum 1 tonne for every 2 hectares)   Supplement: Keep native breeds on grazed habitats (50-80%) £92 per hectare   Supplement: Keep native breeds on grazed habitats (more than 80%) £146 per hectare   Supplement: Keep native breeds on extensively managed habitats (50-80%) £7 per hectare   Supplement: Keep native breeds on extensively management habitats (more than 80%) £11 per hectare   Herbal leys £382 per hectare   Arable reversion to grassland with low fertiliser input £489 per hectare   Manage intensive grassland with low fertiliser input £311 per hectare   Manage grassland to reduce nutrient levels in groundwater £396 per hectare   Supplement: Nil fertiliser £156 per hectare     "
